orgs.Primary Focus: Statewide voter registration, GOTV, coalition coordination (50+ partner orgs)
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: Multiracial democracy; BIPOC voter engagement; voting rights; voter registration; civic infrastructure
orgs.Evidence Summary: Ecosystem-backed, directional — not causally validated at org level. Documented outputs: 122K voters registered, Vote by Mail legislative victory (multi-actor), 2025 PA Supreme Court retention wins (coalition). MVP Neighborhood Captains RCT with David Nickerson underway — results pending.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: The statewide coordinating infrastructure for Pennsylvania's progressive civic ecosystem — a single grant simultaneously activates 50+ partner organizations across the most contested state of the 2026 cycle.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: 1) Regrantor dilution: impact depends on partner quality; no visibility into how PA Voice enforces accountability across 50+ grantees. 2) Rapid revenue growth (3.8→8.6M) could indicate outpacing of internal capacity. 3) Revenue concentration unknown — Schedule B legally redacted.

orgs.Donations Tax Deductible: Unconfirmed — 501(c)(4); possible c3 affiliate; verify before advising donors
orgs.Primary Focus: Volunteer-driven handwritten letters to low-propensity voters; RCT-tested turnout program; repositioning toward new tactic development for 2026
orgs.Issue / Constituency Tags: GOTV; remote volunteering; evidence-based voter contact; low-propensity voters; historically marginalized communities
orgs.States of Operation: National (all states; 2026 priority: PA new voter program, VA redistricting referendum, competitive House districts)
orgs.Evidence Summary: SPLIT RATING. Evidence quality: Tier 1 — 25+ RCTs conducted, 20 of 25 showing positive turnout effects; among the strongest publicly available evidence bases in the field (distinct from Analyst Institute-backed orgs whose results are private). Projected 2026 effectiveness: Tier 2 — Vote Forward itself acknowledges declining effectiveness in high-salience elections including 2024 due to oversaturation. For 2026 competitive House races, channel substitution (simultaneous texts/mail/ads/canvassing), volunteer quality variance, and targeting efficiency concerns all moderate expected impact. Strongest in low-salience, low-information environments.
orgs.One-Sentence Pitch: One of the most rigorously tested remote volunteer programs in the progressive ecosystem — but donors should understand that letter-writing effectiveness has declined in high-salience races, and the 2026 investment case depends on successful new tactic development.
orgs.Key Risks & Flags: 1) TACTIC CONTEXT-DEPENDENCY: Effectiveness Tier 1 historically but Tier 2 projected for 2026 high-salience competitive House races. 2) CHANNEL SUBSTITUTION: In contested districts, marginal letter adds limited incremental signal when competing with texts, mail, ads, canvassing simultaneously. 3) VOLUNTEER QUALITY VARIANCE: 285K distributed volunteers introduce message fidelity variation; RCT conditions may not generalize to real-world deployment. 4) TRANSITION RISK: Repositioning to innovation model introduces 2026 execution uncertainty. 5) IRS STATUS FLAG (URGENT): ProPublica warning must be verified before finalizing recommendation. 6) REVENUE VOLATILITY: Heavy election-cycle dependence; 2025 likely sharp trough. 7) NOT TAX DEDUCTIBLE: 501(c)(4); c3 pathway unconfirmed. 8) INCOMPLETE DILIGENCE: Staff count, program ratio, audit, Candid seal not confirmed.
